about-preview-text: I'm Viktor Bakacs, a software engineer. I build websites, mobile apps and custom software. I focus on clear communication, clean code and solutions that actually work.
why-1-text: Design, development and delivery — no need to coordinate multiple freelancers.
why-2-text: Agile delivery with clear milestones. Your project stays on track and on budget.
why-3-text: Maintainable code, thorough testing and documentation you can rely on long-term.

profile:
About Me

Software engineer with a background in mechanical engineering and automotive software development. I help businesses and teams with web development, custom applications, embedded systems and technical consulting. I value clear communication, clean code and solutions that work in the real world.

blog-subtitle: Thoughts on software engineering, web development and building digital products.
blog-article-1-excerpt: Vteq IT is now live — a new home for my software engineering services, portfolio and client contact.
blog-article-2-title: Why Custom Software Beats Off-the-Shelf Tools
blog-article-2-excerpt: When your workflow is unique, a tailored solution saves time, reduces errors and scales with your business.
blog-article-3-excerpt: Page speed, mobile responsiveness and clean design aren't optional — they're how visitors decide to trust you.
article-1-body:
I'm excited to announce that Vteq IT is now live! This website is my professional home on the web — a place where potential clients can explore my services, browse my portfolio, and get in touch for software engineering projects.

Whether you need a modern website, custom desktop software, embedded systems integration, or technical consulting, this platform makes it easy to start a conversation.

Feel free to explore the site, check out my work, and reach out if you have a project in mind!
contact-page-subtitle: Have a project in mind? Fill out the form and I'll get back to you within 1–2 business days.

portfolio-subtitle: A selection of projects across web, software engineering, UI/UX and embedded systems.
portf-card-testbed: Node-RED + Grafana testbed UI on Raspberry Pi
portf_pg1_h1: Bolt force and tightening torque calculator tool
portf_pg1_p1:
Tools used: C# (WindowsForms), XML (input/output)
OS: Windows, PC
Description: This tool calculates bolt force and tightening torque based on input parameters and generates an XML file with the results.
portf_pg1_h2: Cranktrain force/torque calculator with balancer optimization
portf_pg1_p2:
Tools used: C# (WindowsForms), XML, Matlab, Excel
OS: Windows, PC
Description: Calculates cranktrain force/torque and supports balancer shaft optimization to minimize torques and reset crankshaft forces.
portf_pg1_h3: Testbed manager tool for clutch coupling test
portf_pg1_p3:
Tools used: Node-RED, Python, Grafana, InfluxDB
OS: Linux, Raspberry Pi
Description: UI for a testbed that tests clutch couplings under various conditions. Controls the testbed, collects data, generates reports and transfers results wirelessly.
portf_pg1_h4: Diagnostic status mask generator for trouble codes (DTC)
portf_pg1_p4:
Tools used: Python
OS: Windows, PC
Description: Generates a diagnostic status mask for trouble codes (DTC) based on input parameters.
portf_pg1_p5:
Tools used: Figma
OS: Windows, PC
Description: UI/UX design for a parental control app. Interactive prototype demonstrating workflows, screen designs and user experience concepts.
portf_pg1_p6:
Tools used: HTML, CSS, JavaScript
OS: Web
Description: A simple 2D browser-based game where you avoid obstacles as a dragon.
